The domain defines a rotation process.
Action `rotate`: Rotates the string after a node `?n` by rotation `?r`. It changes the heading of `?n` and sets up the first pass.
Action `rotate-first-pass`: Updates the position and heading of the next node `?n2` in the string, moving the cursor `?n1` to `?n2`. It clears the old position of `?n2`.
Action `rotate-first-pass-end`: Ends the first pass when the end node is reached. Sets up the second pass.
Action `rotate-second-pass`: Sets the correct position for the next node `?n2` based on the position of `?n1` and its heading.
Action `rotate-second-pass-end`: Ends the second pass and clears the `rotating` flag.

4. A sequence of `rotate-second-pass` actions for each node in the string after `n`.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end` action.
